    $obsidian = "$VaultPath\01-Personal-Context\CLAUDE.md"

    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "CLAUDE.md" -ForegroundColor White

    if (-not (Test-Path $source)) {
        Write-Host "  MISSING   : $source — create it first" -ForegroundColor Red
        return
    }

    if (Test-Path $obsidian) {
        if ($Force) {
            Copy-Item $source $obsidian -Force
            Write-Host "  Copied    : ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md -> Obsidian (overwrite)" -ForegroundColor Green
        } else {
            Write-Host "  EXISTS    : Obsidian copy already present. Use -Force to overwrite." -ForegroundColor Yellow
        }
    } else {
        Copy-Item $source $obsidian -Force
        Write-Host "  Copied    : ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md -> Obsidian" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

    Write-Host "  NOTE      : ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is the Claude-side source." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    Write-Host "              Edit in Obsidian, then re-run with -Force to sync back." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
}

# --- Helper: create a plain directory if missing ---
function Ensure-Dir {
    param([string]$Path, [string]$Label)
    if (-not (Test-Path $Path)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $Path -Force | Out-Null
        Write-Host "  Created   : $Label" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    }
}

# --- Create junctions ---

Write-Host "Junctions (~/.claude/ -> Obsidian)" -ForegroundColor White

Set-Junction `
    -LinkPath "$claudePath\commands" `
    -Target   "$VaultPath\04-Commands\_claude-commands"

Set-Junction `
    -LinkPath "$claudePath\skills" `
    -Target   "$VaultPath\03-Skills\_claude-skills"

Set-Junction `
    -LinkPath "$claudePath\learning" `
    -Target   "$VaultPath\02-Learnings\_claude-learning"

Set-Junction `
    -LinkPath "$claudePath\reference" `
    -Target   "$VaultPath\05-Reference\_claude-reference"

# agents/ lives purely in ~/.claude/ — no Obsidian section for it yet
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Runtime directories" -ForegroundColor White
Ensure-Dir "$claudePath\agents"    "agents/"
Ensure-Dir "$claudePath\state"     "state/"
Ensure-Dir "$claudePath\worktrees" "worktrees/"

# --- CLAUDE.md ---
Set-ClaudeMd

# --- Verification ---
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Verification" -ForegroundColor White

$checks = @(
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\commands"; Label = "commands" },
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\skills";   Label = "skills"   },
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\learning"; Label = "learning" },
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\reference";Label = "reference"},
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\agents";   Label = "agents"   },
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\state";    Label = "state"    },
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\worktrees";Label = "worktrees"},
    @{ Path = "$claudePath\CLAUDE.md";Label = "CLAUDE.md"}
)

$allOk = $true
foreach ($c in $checks) {
    if (Test-Path $c.Path) {
        $item = Get-Item $c.Path -Force
        $tag = if ($item.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint) { "junction" } else { "real" }
        Write-Host "  OK  [$tag] $($c.Label)" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        Write-Host "  MISSING    $($c.Label)" -ForegroundColor Red
        $allOk = $false
    }
}

Write-Host ""
if ($allOk) {
    Write-Host "All links verified. Claude Code will read from your Obsidian vault." -ForegroundColor Cyan
} else {
    Write-Host "Some items missing. Re-run with -Force or check errors above." -ForegroundColor Yellow
}
Write-Host ""

toolUseResult.originalFile:
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Sets up junctions from ~/.claude/ into the Obsidian vault.
    Obsidian is the source of truth. Claude Code reads via junctions.

.DESCRIPTION
    Direction: ~/.claude/{dir} -> junction -> D:\OneDrive\...\01_Claude_Home\{section}\_claude-{dir}
    This means editing in Obsidian is immediately reflected in Claude sessions.
    On a new machine: wait for OneDrive sync, then run this script once.

.PARAMETER VaultPath
    Path to the Obsidian vault folder. Defaults to the correct OneDrive location.

.PARAMETER Force
    Remove and recreate existing junctions.

.EXAMPLE
    .\setup-links.ps1
    .\setup-links.ps1 -Force
    .\setup-links.ps1 -VaultPath "E:\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\01_Claude_Home"
#>

param(
    [string]$VaultPath = "D:\OneDrive\16 - Obsidian\01_Claude_Home",
    [switch]$Force
)

$claudePath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Claude Code — Personal Vault Link Setup" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Claude dir : $claudePath" -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host "  Vault      : $VaultPath" -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host ""

# --- Preflight checks ---

if (-not (Test-Path $claudePath)) {
    Write-Host "ERROR: Claude directory not found at $claudePath" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "       Install Claude Code first (winget install Anthropic.ClaudeCode)" -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

if (-not (Test-Path $VaultPath)) {
    Write-Host "ERROR: Vault not found at $VaultPath" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "       Wait for OneDrive to sync, or pass -VaultPath with the correct path." -ForegroundColor Red
    exit 1
}

# --- Helper: create or verify a junction ---
function Set-Junction {
    param(
        [string]$LinkPath,   # ~/.claude/{dir}  (the junction to create)
        [string]$Target      # Obsidian subfolder (real directory, source of truth)
    )

    $name = Split-Path $LinkPath -Leaf

    # Ensure the real target directory exists in Obsidian
    if (-not (Test-Path $Target)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $Target -Force | Out-Null
        Write-Host "  Created   : $Target" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    }

    # Remove existing link/dir if -Force
    if (Test-Path $LinkPath) {
        $existing = Get-Item $LinkPath -Force
        $isJunction = $existing.Attributes -band [IO.FileAttributes]::ReparsePoint
        if ($Force) {
            if ($isJunction) {
                # Remove junction without deleting contents
                cmd /c rmdir "$LinkPath" 2>&1 | Out-Null
                Write-Host "  Removed   : existing junction $name" -ForegroundColor DarkGray
            } else {
                Write-Host "  SKIP      : $name exists and is a real directory (not a junction). Remove manually to replace." -ForegroundColor Yellow
                return
            }
        } else {
            if ($isJunction) {
                Write-Host "  OK        : $name (junction already exists)" -ForegroundColor Green
            } else {
                Write-Host "  SKIP      : $name exists as a real directory. Use -Force to replace." -ForegroundColor Yellow
            }
            return
        }
    }

    # Create junction (works without admin, works cross-drive on Windows)
    $result = cmd /c mklink /J "$LinkPath" "$Target" 2>&1
    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
        Write-Host "  Linked    : $name -> $Target" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        Write-Host "  FAILED    : $name -> $result" -ForegroundColor Red
    }
}

    Write-Host "CLAUDE.md" -ForegroundColor White

    }

    if (Test-Path $obsidian) {

        } else {

        }
    } else {
        Copy-Item $source $obsidian -Force
        Write-Host "  Copied    : ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md -> Obsidian" -ForegroundColor Green
    }

    Write-Host "  NOTE      : ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is the Claude-side source." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
    Write-Host "              Edit in Obsidian, then re-run with -Force to sync back." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
}

function Ensure-Dir {
    param([string]$Path, [string]$Label)

## Result

**Junctions created** (Obsidian = source of truth, `~/.claude/` reads through them):

|---|---|---|

- `~/.claude/state/` — session phase tracking
- `~/.claude/worktrees/` — RPI task artifacts

# Personal Claude Environment - Eduardo Marquez

Last updated: 2026-05-08

This is your personal Claude Code configuration, separate from Safari Circuits work content.

---

## About This Setup

This repository provides:
- **Enterprise layer** - Reusable patterns across all your personal projects
- **User layer** - Eduardo-specific preferences and context
- **Git versioning** - Track how your Claude setup evolves
- **Multi-computer sync** - Same setup on home and other personal computers

**Source:** `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs`

---

## Core Principles

1. **Capture as you learn** - Document insights when you discover them
2. **Build reusable skills** - Turn repeated patterns into skills
3. **Keep it simple** - Minimal structure, maximum value
4. **Version everything** - Commit learnings and skills to git
5. **Evolve continuously** - Review and refine weekly/monthly

---

## Folder Structure

```
.claude/enterprise/              (Reusable across all personal projects)
├── learning/
│   ├── best-practices/         (Validated knowledge)
│   └── ideas/                  (Idea backlog)
├── skills/                     (Reusable workflows)
├── commands/                   (Custom slash commands)
├── reference/                  (Quick lookup docs)
└── agents/                     (AI subagents)

.claude/users/emarquez-personal/ (Personal preferences)
├── skills/                     (Personal-only skills)
├── commands/                   (Personal-only commands)
└── hooks/                      (Custom hooks)
```

---

## My Preferences

### Work Style

**Decision-Making:**
- Present 2-3 options with clear tradeoffs
- Flag risks proactively
- I prefer "here's what could go wrong" over optimistic estimates

**Communication:**
- Brief summaries first, details on request
- Use tables for comparisons
- Technical depth is fine - I'm hands-on

**Time & Focus:**
- Work in 1-2 hour focused blocks
- Context switches are normal
- End-of-day: prefer quick wins over starting large tasks

**Things I Value:**
- Clear tradeoff explanations
- Proactive risk identification
- Learning from mistakes (capture in learnings)
- Incremental progress over big-bang changes

**Things That Frustrate Me:**
- Surprises in production
- Documentation drift
- Over-engineering when simple works

---

## Technologies I Use

### Languages
- **Primary:** Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, SQL
- **Learning:** Go, Rust (future)

### Frameworks & Libraries
- **Backend:** FastAPI, Express, Flask
- **Frontend:** React, Next.js
- **Data:** Pandas, NumPy

### Databases
- **SQL:** PostgreSQL, SQL Server
- **NoSQL:** MongoDB, Redis

### Cloud & Infrastructure
- **Cloud:** Azure (primary), AWS (learning)
- **Containers:** Docker, Docker Compose
- **IaC:** Terraform (learning)

### Tools
- **Editor:** VS Code, Claude Code
- **Version Control:** Git, GitHub
- **Notes:** Obsidian
- **Terminal:** PowerShell, bash

---

## Common Tasks

### Things I Build Often
- REST APIs with authentication
- Data processing pipelines
- Admin dashboards
- Database schemas and migrations
- Automation scripts

### Patterns I Use
- Repository pattern for data access
- Dependency injection
- API versioning
- Error handling middleware
- Database transactions

---

## Learning Goals (2026)

**Current Focus:**
- [ ] Advanced TypeScript patterns
- [ ] Docker orchestration and Kubernetes basics
- [ ] System design patterns
- [ ] Performance optimization techniques

**Future Quarters:**
- [ ] Go for backend services
- [ ] GraphQL APIs
- [ ] Event-driven architecture
- [ ] Distributed systems patterns

---

## Project Types

### Personal Projects
- Side projects and experiments
- Learning exercises
- Open source contributions
- Personal automation tools

### Home Lab
- Home server setup
- Network automation
- Self-hosted services
- Infrastructure experiments

### Study & Research
- Technology research
- API explorations
- Performance testing
- Architecture design practice

---

## Coding Standards (Personal)

### General Principles
- **Readability first** - Code is read more than written
- **Simple over clever** - Clever code is hard to maintain
- **Test what matters** - Focus on business logic, not trivial code
- **Document why, not what** - Code shows what, comments explain why

### TypeScript/JavaScript
- Use `type` over `interface`
- Prefer functional patterns where appropriate
- Use `const` by default, `let` when needed, never `var`
- Avoid `any` - use `unknown` if type is truly unknown

### Python
- Follow PEP 8
- Type hints on functions (params + return)
- Use dataclasses for data structures
- Prefer list comprehensions for simple transformations

### SQL
- Use explicit JOINs (never implicit)
- Always use transactions for multi-statement operations
- Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN
- Comment complex queries

### Git
- Commit messages: imperative mood ("Add feature" not "Added feature")
- Small, focused commits
- Branch naming: `feature/`, `fix/`, `refactor/`
- Rebase before merge to keep history clean

---

## Custom Commands

*(Add your custom /commands as you create them)*

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/review` | Review code changes before commit |
| `/learn` | Capture a learning in best-practices/ |
| `/quickfix` | Quick bug fix workflow |

---

## Maintenance Schedule

### Weekly (Friday Evening)
- Review `learning/ideas/backlog.md`
- Capture any learnings from the week
- Update skills that evolved
- Check learning goals progress

### Monthly (First Weekend)
- Review and update this CLAUDE.md
- Archive obsolete content
- Update learning goals
- Review project progress

### Quarterly
- Deep review of all learnings
- Promote patterns to skills
- Update technology focus areas
- Plan next quarter's goals

---

## Claude Behavior

### Auto-Capture Patterns

When I say these phrases, help me capture knowledge:
- "I learned that..." → Suggest capturing in learning/best-practices/
- "I keep doing..." → Suggest creating a skill
- "Next time I should..." → Capture in ideas/backlog.md

### Correction Handling

When I correct you:
1. Acknowledge the correction
2. Offer to capture it as a learning
3. Apply it going forward

### Workflow Assistance

- **Planning:** Help break down large tasks into steps
- **Research:** Explore technologies/patterns before building
- **Review:** Check my code for issues before commit
- **Learning:** Help document what I discover

---

## Context Review

Use these prompts for regular reviews:

### Weekly Review
```
Let's do my weekly review:
1. What learnings should I capture from this week?
2. Any skills that need updating?
3. Check learning goals progress
```

### Monthly Review
```
Monthly Claude environment review:
1. Review learning/ideas/backlog.md
2. Update CLAUDE.md preferences
3. Archive obsolete content
4. Update learning goals
```

---

## Notes for Claude

- This is personal use only (no team/work content)
- I'm building this system as I go - suggestions welcome
- When I correct you, auto-propose capturing the learning
- Focus on practical, reusable knowledge
- Keep things simple and maintainable

---

## Repository Management

**Git workflow:**
- Commit learnings and skills as they're created
- Use descriptive commit messages
- Tag major milestones (quarterly reviews, significant additions)

**Branching:**
- `main` - stable, tested content
- `dev` - work in progress
- `experiment/` - trying new patterns

**Backup:**
- Push to GitHub regularly
- This repo syncs to all personal computers via git

---

## Getting Started Checklist

- [x] Repository created and initialized
- [x] Folder structure in place
- [x] CLAUDE.md created
- [ ] First learning captured
- [ ] First skill created
- [ ] First command added
- [ ] Bootstrap script run on home computer
- [ ] Pushed to GitHub

---

## Sources & Inspiration

- Safari Circuits team standards (organizational-docs)
- Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow
- HumanLayer's Context Engineering patterns
- Personal experience and preferences

content.content:
1	#Requires -Version 5.1
2	<#
3	.SYNOPSIS
4	    Bootstrap Personal Claude Environment
5	
6	.DESCRIPTION
7	    Creates symlinks from ~/.claude/ to personal-claude-docs repository.
8	
9	    This script:
10	    - Backs up existing .claude files if needed
11	    - Creates symlinks to enterprise layer (learning, skills, commands, etc.)
12	    - Creates symlinks to user layer (personal preferences)
13	    - Verifies setup
14	
15	.PARAMETER Force
16	    Remove existing symlinks and recreate them
17	
18	.PARAMETER BackupExisting
19	    Backup existing .claude content before creating symlinks
20	
21	.EXAMPLE
22	    .\setup-personal.ps1
23	
24	.EXAMPLE
25	    .\setup-personal.ps1 -Force -BackupExisting
26	#>
27	
28	[CmdletBinding()]
29	param(
30	    [Parameter()]
31	    [switch]$Force,
32	
33	    [Parameter()]
34	    [switch]$BackupExisting
35	)
36	
37	$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
38	
39	# Colors
40	function Write-Step { param([string]$Message) Write-Host "→ $Message" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
41	function Write-Success { param([string]$Message) Write-Host "✓ $Message" -ForegroundColor Green }
42	function Write-Warning { param([string]$Message) Write-Host "⚠ $Message" -ForegroundColor Yellow }
43	function Write-Fail { param([string]$Message) Write-Host "✗ $Message" -ForegroundColor Red }
44	
45	# Header
46	Write-Host "`n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" -ForegroundColor Blue
47	Write-Host "║   Personal Claude Environment Setup                  ║" -ForegroundColor Blue
48	Write-Host "║   Eduardo Marquez - personal-claude-docs             ║" -ForegroundColor Blue
49	Write-Host "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝`n" -ForegroundColor Blue
50	
51	# Paths
52	$claudeRoot = "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
53	$repoRoot = Split-Path (Split-Path $PSScriptRoot -Parent) -Parent
54	$enterpriseSource = Join-Path $repoRoot ".claude\enterprise"
55	$userSource = Join-Path $repoRoot ".claude\users\emarquez-personal"
56	
57	Write-Step "Validating paths..."
58	Write-Host "  Claude root: $claudeRoot"
59	Write-Host "  Repository: $repoRoot"
60	Write-Host "  Enterprise source: $enterpriseSource"
61	Write-Host "  User source: $userSource"
62	
63	# Validate
64	if (-not (Test-Path $claudeRoot)) {
65	    Write-Fail "Claude directory not found at $claudeRoot"
66	    Write-Host "Please run Claude Code at least once to initialize ~/.claude/" -ForegroundColor Yellow
67	    exit 1
68	}
69	
70	if (-not (Test-Path $enterpriseSource)) {
71	    Write-Fail "Enterprise source not found at $enterpriseSource"
72	    Write-Host "Make sure you're running this script from personal-claude-docs/.claude/scripts/" -ForegroundColor Yellow
73	    exit 1
74	}
75	
76	# Backup existing content if requested
77	if ($BackupExisting) {
78	    Write-Step "Backing up existing .claude content..."
79	    $backupDir = Join-Path $claudeRoot "backup-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')"
80	
81	    $foldersToBackup = @("learning", "skills", "commands", "reference", "agents")
82	    $needsBackup = $false
83	
84	    foreach ($folder in $foldersToBackup) {
85	        $path = Join-Path $claudeRoot $folder
86	        if (Test-Path $path) {
87	            if (-not $needsBackup) {
88	                New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $backupDir -Force | Out-Null
89	                $needsBackup = $true
90	            }
91	            Copy-Item -Path $path -Destination $backupDir -Recurse -Force
92	            Write-Success "Backed up: $folder"
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	    if ($needsBackup) {
97	        Write-Success "Backup saved to: $backupDir"
98	    } else {
99	        Write-Host "  Nothing to backup" -ForegroundColor Gray
100	    }
101	}
102	
103	# Define symlinks
104	$symlinks = @(
105	    @{
106	        Name = "learning"
107	        Source = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "learning"
108	        Target = Join-Path $claudeRoot "learning"
109	        Description = "Learning and best practices"
110	    },
111	    @{
112	        Name = "skills"
113	        Source = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "skills"
114	        Target = Join-Path $claudeRoot "skills"
115	        Description = "Reusable skills"
116	    },
117	    @{
118	        Name = "commands"
119	        Source = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "commands"
120	        Target = Join-Path $claudeRoot "commands"
121	        Description = "Custom commands"
122	    },
123	    @{
124	        Name = "reference"
125	        Source = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "reference"
126	        Target = Join-Path $claudeRoot "reference"
127	        Description = "Quick reference docs"
128	    },
129	    @{
130	        Name = "agents"
131	        Source = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "agents"
132	        Target = Join-Path $claudeRoot "agents"
133	        Description = "AI subagents"
134	    }
135	)
136	
137	# Create symlinks
138	Write-Step "Creating symlinks from .claude to personal-claude-docs..."
139	
140	foreach ($link in $symlinks) {
141	    $targetPath = $link.Target
142	    $sourcePath = $link.Source
143	
144	    # Check if target exists
145	    if (Test-Path $targetPath) {
146	        if ($Force) {
147	            Write-Warning "Removing existing: $($link.Name)"
148	            Remove-Item $targetPath -Force -Recurse
149	        } else {
150	            Write-Host "  Already exists: $($link.Name)" -ForegroundColor Gray
151	            continue
152	        }
153	    }
154	
155	    # Verify source exists
156	    if (-not (Test-Path $sourcePath)) {
157	        Write-Warning "Source not found: $sourcePath"
158	        continue
159	    }
160	
161	    # Create symlink
162	    try {
163	        New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path $targetPath -Target $sourcePath -Force | Out-Null
164	        Write-Success "Linked: $($link.Name) → personal-claude-docs"
165	    } catch {
166	        Write-Fail "Failed to create symlink for $($link.Name): $_"
167	    }
168	}
169	
170	# Handle CLAUDE.md
171	Write-Step "Setting up CLAUDE.md..."
172	
173	$claudeMdSource = Join-Path $enterpriseSource "CLAUDE.md"
174	$claudeMdTarget = Join-Path $claudeRoot "CLAUDE.md"
175	
176	if (Test-Path $claudeMdTarget) {
177	    if ($BackupExisting) {
178	        $backupPath = "$claudeMdTarget.backup-$(Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmmss')"
179	        Copy-Item $claudeMdTarget $backupPath
180	        Write-Success "Backed up existing CLAUDE.md"
181	    }
182	
183	    if ($Force) {
184	        Remove-Item $claudeMdTarget -Force
185	    } else {
186	        Write-Warning "CLAUDE.md already exists (keeping existing)"
187	        Write-Host "  Run with -Force to replace" -ForegroundColor Yellow
188	    }
189	}
190	
191	if (-not (Test-Path $claudeMdTarget)) {
192	    New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path $claudeMdTarget -Target $claudeMdSource -Force | Out-Null
193	    Write-Success "Linked: CLAUDE.md → personal-claude-docs"
194	}
195	
196	# Verify setup
197	Write-Step "Verifying setup..."
198	
199	$allGood = $true
200	foreach ($link in $symlinks) {
201	    if (Test-Path $link.Target) {
202	        $item = Get-Item $link.Target
203	        if ($item.LinkType -eq "SymbolicLink") {
204	            Write-Host "  ✓ $($link.Name)" -ForegroundColor Green
205	        } else {
206	            Write-Host "  ✗ $($link.Name) (not a symlink)" -ForegroundColor Red
207	            $allGood = $false
208	        }
209	    } else {
210	        Write-Host "  ✗ $($link.Name) (missing)" -ForegroundColor Red
211	        $allGood = $false
212	    }
213	}
214	
215	# Summary
216	Write-Host "`n╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" -ForegroundColor Green
217	Write-Host "║   ✓ Personal Claude Environment Ready                ║" -ForegroundColor Green
218	Write-Host "╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝`n" -ForegroundColor Green
219	
220	Write-Host "Setup complete!" -ForegroundColor Cyan
221	Write-Host "  • Files live in: " -NoNewline; Write-Host $repoRoot -ForegroundColor Yellow
222	Write-Host "  • Claude reads from: " -NoNewline; Write-Host $claudeRoot -ForegroundColor Yellow
223	Write-Host "  • Edit in repo, Claude uses automatically" -ForegroundColor Gray
224	
225	Write-Host "`nWhat's available:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
226	Write-Host "  • learning/    - Capture knowledge and best practices"
227	Write-Host "  • skills/      - Build reusable workflows"
228	Write-Host "  • commands/    - Create custom /commands"
229	Write-Host "  • reference/   - Quick lookup docs"
230	Write-Host "  • agents/      - Specialized AI subagents"
231	
232	Write-Host "`nNext steps:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
233	Write-Host "  1. Read: $repoRoot\README.md"
234	Write-Host "  2. Review: $claudeRoot\CLAUDE.md"
235	Write-Host "  3. Start using Claude Code - it will load your personal context"
236	Write-Host "  4. Capture your first learning in learning/best-practices/"
237	Write-Host "  5. Commit and push to GitHub for backup"
238	
239	Write-Host "`nGit workflow:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
240	Write-Host "  cd $repoRoot"
241	Write-Host "  git add ."
242	Write-Host '  git commit -m "Add: first learning"'
243	Write-Host "  git push"
244	
245	if (-not $allGood) {
246	    Write-Host "`n⚠ Some symlinks failed to create. Run with -Force to fix." -ForegroundColor Yellow
247	    exit 1
248	}
249	
250	Write-Host ""
251

Good. Now I have the full picture. The existing files are a solid start but missing the full Boris Cherny workflow. Here's what needs updating across the repo:

1. `enterprise/CLAUDE.md` — rewrite with complete workflow + multi-computer architecture
2. `users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md` — create with Eduardo's preferences
3. `scripts/setup-personal.ps1` — add Obsidian vault symlinks, `enterprise`/`personal` dirs, computer detection, junction fallback
4. `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` — thin loader using `@enterprise/CLAUDE.md`
5. Rewire `~/.claude/` symlinks from Obsidian → personal-claude-docs

Let me do all of these:

input.content:
# Personal Claude Code — Enterprise Configuration

> Shared across all personal computers via ClaudeDocs (`personal-claude-docs` repo).
> Loaded by `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` on every session via `@enterprise/CLAUDE.md`.

---

## Multi-Computer Architecture

This configuration is shared across all personal computers through `ClaudeDocs`.
Each machine has a thin `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` that loads this file and the personal layer.

```
ClaudeDocs repo (personal-claude-docs/)
├── .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md    ← This file. Portable, machine-agnostic.
├── .claude/enterprise/commands/    ← ~/.claude/commands → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/skills/      ← ~/.claude/skills → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/learning/    ← ~/.claude/learning → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/reference/   ← ~/.claude/reference → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/agents/      ← ~/.claude/agents → symlink → here
├── .claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md  ← Personal preferences layer
└── .claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1         ← Run once per machine
```

### Loading Order (every Claude session)

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  @enterprise/CLAUDE.md   ← this file  (Boris Cherny practices, workflow)
  @personal/CLAUDE.md     ← personal preferences, tech stack, current focus
  ## Machine-specific     ← local paths, nothing else
```

### Machines

| Machine | User | OneDrive | ClaudeDocs | Claude repo |
|---------|------|----------|------------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | `C:\Users\eduar\personal-claude-docs\` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | `C:\Users\{user}\personal-claude-docs\` | — |

### New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone repos
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"   # or ClaudeWork

# 2. Run bootstrap (auto-detects home vs work by OneDrive location)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 3. Verify
claude  # CLAUDE.md loads, commands/skills available
```

### Work Computer Layering

On the work computer, `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (ClaudeWork repo) loads:
1. `@enterprise/CLAUDE.md` — this file (personal practices)
2. `@personal/CLAUDE.md` — personal preferences
3. Work-specific section — Safari Circuits, ADO, BeFirst context

Personal context always loads first and sets the baseline behavior.

---

## Core Principles (Always Follow)

1. **Think first, read before touching** — Investigate relevant files BEFORE making changes. Never speculate about code you haven't opened.
2. **Check in before major changes** — Present plan and get confirmation before significant modifications.
3. **Minimal changes only** — Each change should impact as little code as possible. No scope creep.
4. **Verify after every change** — Run tests, typecheck, lint after each modification.
5. **Explain at a high level** — Concise summary of what changed and why. No narration of internal process.
6. **Never speculate** — If you haven't read a file, say so. Don't make claims about unread code.
7. **Keep docs current** — Update CLAUDE.md with learnings when mistakes are made.
8. **Self-improving** — Every correction is a learning opportunity. Propose captures proactively.
9. **Automatic workflow** — Session management happens automatically. User just works.

---

## CLAUDE.md Hierarchy

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md           ← Global personal config (always loaded)
  ↓ @enterprise/CLAUDE.md     ← This file
  ↓ @personal/CLAUDE.md       ← Personal preferences
{project}/CLAUDE.md           ← Project-specific (loaded per-project)
{project}/{subdir}/CLAUDE.md  ← Subdirectory overrides
```

- Use `/init` in any new project to auto-generate its CLAUDE.md
- Project CLAUDE.md: codebase overview, build commands, key paths, gotchas
- Use `@filename` in conversation or CLAUDE.md to inject file contents

---

## Session Workflow (Automatic)

```
[First message] → START → WORK → CHECKPOINT → VALIDATE → [Session ends] → CLOSE
```

| Phase | Trigger | Action (silent) |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| **Start** | First message | Load state, assess context health |
| **Work** | User requests | Plan mode for complex tasks, incremental changes |
| **Checkpoint** | ~40 turns or topic shift | Compress context, update state |
| **Validate** | After code changes | Run tests/lint |
| **Close** | "done", "bye" | Full checkpoint, propose learning captures |

### Context Health

| Usage | State | Action |
|-------|-------|--------|
| 0–40% | Smart Zone | Continue |
| 40–60% | Degrading | `/compact`, checkpoint to file |
| >60% | Dumb Zone | Stop — start fresh with artifacts |

**`/compact`** compresses context while preserving key facts. Use it proactively.

---

## Context Maturation Pipeline

Every session should advance knowledge along this lifecycle:

```
Idea → Learning → Skill → Standard → Governance
  ↑                                      |
  └──────────────── Feedback ────────────┘
```

| Stage | Location | Promotion Trigger |
|-------|----------|-------------------|
| **Idea** | `learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Reviewed and validated |
| **Learning** | `learning/best-practices/` | Used successfully 3+ times |
| **Skill** | `skills/{name}/SKILL.md` | Stable, documented, tested |
| **Standard** | CLAUDE.md Learnings section | Applied consistently |

### Auto-Capture Triggers

| User Says | Claude Does |
|-----------|-------------|
| "I learned that..." | Offer to capture in `learning/ideas/backlog.md` |
| "I keep doing..." | Suggest creating a skill |
| "Next time I should..." | Capture in ideas backlog |
| Any correction | Propose CLAUDE.md learning addition |
| Pattern repeats 3+ times | Suggest formalizing as a skill |

---

## Research → Plan → Implement (RPI)

For non-trivial tasks, use structured phases:

```
/research → research.md → /plan → plan.md → /implement → code
     ↓                       ↓                   ↓
 Sub-agents          Human Review Gate      Checkpoints
```

| Complexity | Criteria | Approach |
|------------|----------|----------|
| **Simple** | Single file, clear change | Standard execution |
| **Medium** | Multi-file, feature work | `/research` → `/plan` → `/implement` |
| **Complex** | Large refactor, architecture | Full RPI with subagents |

**One bad research line → 10 bad plan lines → 100 bad code lines.**
Always get human approval at Plan phase before implementing.

Subagents for discovery (keeps main context clean):
- `agents/codebase-locator.md` — find WHERE files live
- `agents/codebase-analyzer.md` — understand HOW code works
- `agents/pattern-finder.md` — find existing patterns to follow

Task artifacts → `~/.claude/worktrees/{task-id}/`

---

## Custom Slash Commands

Commands in `~/.claude/commands/*.md` → `/command-name` in any session.

### Built-in Claude Code Commands

| Command | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `/init` | Auto-generate CLAUDE.md for current project |
| `/compact` | Compress context window |
| `/clear` | Reset session context entirely |
| `/fast` | Toggle fast mode |
| `/memory` | View/edit persistent memory |

### Personal Commands (add as you create them)

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/start-work` | Load session context, check state |
| `/end-work` | Full checkpoint, capture learnings |
| `/checkpoint` | Mid-session state save |
| `/status` | Where am I, what's in progress |
| `/research` | Spawn subagents for discovery |
| `/plan` | Structure implementation plan (needs approval) |
| `/implement` | Execute approved plan |
| `/validate-work` | Run tests, lint, typecheck |
| `/quick-commit` | Stage, commit, push with smart message |
| `/brain-dump` | Capture unstructured thoughts |
| `/pulse` | Mid-session health check |
| `/learn` | Capture a learning |

---

## Natural Language → Command Mappings

| User Says | Execute |
|-----------|---------|
| "start work", "let's get started" | `/start-work` |
| "end work", "wrap up", "done for now" | `/end-work` |
| "what's the status" | `/status` |
| "validate", "check everything" | `/validate-work` |
| "commit and push" | `/quick-commit` |
| "brain dump" | `/brain-dump` |
| "reminder:", "don't forget" | `/reminder` |
| "checkpoint", "save progress" | `/checkpoint` |
| "research this", "investigate" | `/research` |
| "create a plan" | `/plan` |
| "implement the plan" | `/implement` |
| "pulse", "am I on track" | `/pulse` |
| "I learned that..." | Offer to capture learning |

---

## Hooks System

Configure in `~/.claude/settings.json` under `"hooks"`. Personal hooks live in
`~/.claude/personal/hooks/` (symlink to `users/emarquez-personal/hooks/`).

| Event | Fires When | Good For |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `PreToolUse` | Before any tool call | Validation, safety checks |
| `PostToolUse` | After any tool call | Auto-format, notifications |
| `Stop` | Claude finishes responding | Checkpoint, post-session cleanup |

---

## Auto-Memory System

Persistent memory across sessions at `~/.claude/projects/{path}/memory/`.
Loaded automatically. Separate from `learning/` (memory = Claude's working knowledge; learning/ = curated knowledge base).

| Type | Content |
|------|---------|
| `user_*.md` | Who you are, expertise, preferences |
| `feedback_*.md` | Corrections and validated approaches |
| `project_*.md` | Ongoing work, goals, deadlines |
| `reference_*.md` | Where to find things externally |

`MEMORY.md` = index file. Keep under 200 lines.

---

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- Starting to code without reading existing code first
- Making "while I'm here" scope-creep changes
- Skipping verification (tests/lint) after changes
- Speculating about code behavior without reading it
- Large changes mixing unrelated concerns
- Writing comments that explain WHAT (code is self-documenting) — only write WHY
- Adding error handling for impossible scenarios
- Creating new files when editing existing ones works

---

## Git Workflow for This Repo

```bash
# After adding learnings, skills, or commands:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/  .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: [what you discovered]"
git push

# On another computer after pulling:
git pull
# No re-run of setup needed - symlinks already point here
```

---

## Maintenance Schedule

| Cadence | Action |
|---------|--------|
| **Weekly** | Review `learning/ideas/backlog.md`, capture week's learnings, commit |
| **Monthly** | Update personal/CLAUDE.md goals, archive stale content, review skills |
| **Quarterly** | Deep review, promote patterns to skills, plan next quarter |

---

## Learnings & Corrections

*Format: `[YYYY-MM-DD] Context: Learning.`*

- [2026-05-08] Architecture: `personal-claude-docs` is the shared personal baseline. `~/.claude/` is machine-specific. Obsidian vaults are OneDrive-only (not git). Symlinks connect all three.
- [2026-05-08] CLAUDE.md loading: `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` uses `@enterprise/CLAUDE.md` and `@personal/CLAUDE.md` to load shared content. Enterprise symlink at `~/.claude/enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/`.
- [2026-05-08] Hooks: Configure in `settings.json`. Fire on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop. Use for auto-format, git checkpoints, desktop notifications.

---

## Sources

- [Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179832300581177)
- [Claude Code documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/claude-code)
- [Dex Horthy / HumanLayer — Advanced Context Engineering](https://github.com/humanlayer/advanced-context-engineering-for-coding-agents)
- [12-Factor Agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

4. **Verify after every change** — Run tests, typecheck, lint after each modification.
5. **Explain at a high level** — Concise summary of what changed and why. No narration of internal process.
6. **Never speculate** — If you haven't read a file, say so. Don't make claims about unread code.
7. **Keep docs current** — Update CLAUDE.md with learnings when mistakes are made.
8. **Self-improving** — Every correction is a learning opportunity. Propose captures proactively.
9. **Automatic workflow** — Session management happens automatically. User just works.

---

## CLAUDE.md Hierarchy

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md           ← Global personal config (always loaded)
  ↓ @enterprise/CLAUDE.md     ← This file
  ↓ @personal/CLAUDE.md       ← Personal preferences
{project}/CLAUDE.md           ← Project-specific (loaded per-project)
{project}/{subdir}/CLAUDE.md  ← Subdirectory overrides
```

- Use `/init` in any new project to auto-generate its CLAUDE.md
- Project CLAUDE.md: codebase overview, build commands, key paths, gotchas
- Use `@filename` in conversation or CLAUDE.md to inject file contents

---

## Session Workflow (Automatic)

```
[First message] → START → WORK → CHECKPOINT → VALIDATE → [Session ends] → CLOSE
```

| Phase | Trigger | Action (silent) |
|-------|---------|----------------|
| **Start** | First message | Load state, assess context health |
| **Work** | User requests | Plan mode for complex tasks, incremental changes |
| **Checkpoint** | ~40 turns or topic shift | Compress context, update state |
| **Validate** | After code changes | Run tests/lint |
| **Close** | "done", "bye" | Full checkpoint, propose learning captures |

### Context Health

| Usage | State | Action |
|-------|-------|--------|
| 0–40% | Smart Zone | Continue |
| 40–60% | Degrading | `/compact`, checkpoint to file |
| >60% | Dumb Zone | Stop — start fresh with artifacts |

**`/compact`** compresses context while preserving key facts. Use it proactively.

---

## Context Maturation Pipeline

Every session should advance knowledge along this lifecycle:

```
Idea → Learning → Skill → Standard → Governance
  ↑                                      |
  └──────────────── Feedback ────────────┘
```

| Stage | Location | Promotion Trigger |
|-------|----------|-------------------|
| **Idea** | `learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Reviewed and validated |
| **Learning** | `learning/best-practices/` | Used successfully 3+ times |
| **Skill** | `skills/{name}/SKILL.md` | Stable, documented, tested |
| **Standard** | CLAUDE.md Learnings section | Applied consistently |

### Auto-Capture Triggers

| User Says | Claude Does |
|-----------|-------------|
| "I learned that..." | Offer to capture in `learning/ideas/backlog.md` |
| "I keep doing..." | Suggest creating a skill |
| "Next time I should..." | Capture in ideas backlog |
| Any correction | Propose CLAUDE.md learning addition |
| Pattern repeats 3+ times | Suggest formalizing as a skill |

---

## Research → Plan → Implement (RPI)

For non-trivial tasks, use structured phases:

```
/research → research.md → /plan → plan.md → /implement → code
     ↓                       ↓                   ↓
 Sub-agents          Human Review Gate      Checkpoints
```

| Complexity | Criteria | Approach |
|------------|----------|----------|
| **Simple** | Single file, clear change | Standard execution |
| **Medium** | Multi-file, feature work | `/research` → `/plan` → `/implement` |
| **Complex** | Large refactor, architecture | Full RPI with subagents |

**One bad research line → 10 bad plan lines → 100 bad code lines.**
Always get human approval at Plan phase before implementing.

Subagents for discovery (keeps main context clean):
- `agents/codebase-locator.md` — find WHERE files live
- `agents/codebase-analyzer.md` — understand HOW code works
- `agents/pattern-finder.md` — find existing patterns to follow

Task artifacts → `~/.claude/worktrees/{task-id}/`

---

## Custom Slash Commands

Commands in `~/.claude/commands/*.md` → `/command-name` in any session.

### Built-in Claude Code Commands

| Command | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `/init` | Auto-generate CLAUDE.md for current project |
| `/compact` | Compress context window |
| `/clear` | Reset session context entirely |
| `/fast` | Toggle fast mode |
| `/memory` | View/edit persistent memory |

### Personal Commands (add as you create them)

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/start-work` | Load session context, check state |
| `/end-work` | Full checkpoint, capture learnings |
| `/checkpoint` | Mid-session state save |
| `/status` | Where am I, what's in progress |
| `/research` | Spawn subagents for discovery |
| `/plan` | Structure implementation plan (needs approval) |
| `/implement` | Execute approved plan |
| `/validate-work` | Run tests, lint, typecheck |
| `/quick-commit` | Stage, commit, push with smart message |
| `/brain-dump` | Capture unstructured thoughts |
| `/pulse` | Mid-session health check |
| `/learn` | Capture a learning |

---

## Natural Language → Command Mappings

| User Says | Execute |
|-----------|---------|
| "start work", "let's get started" | `/start-work` |
| "end work", "wrap up", "done for now" | `/end-work` |
| "what's the status" | `/status` |
| "validate", "check everything" | `/validate-work` |
| "commit and push" | `/quick-commit` |
| "brain dump" | `/brain-dump` |
| "reminder:", "don't forget" | `/reminder` |
| "checkpoint", "save progress" | `/checkpoint` |
| "research this", "investigate" | `/research` |
| "create a plan" | `/plan` |
| "implement the plan" | `/implement` |
| "pulse", "am I on track" | `/pulse` |
| "I learned that..." | Offer to capture learning |

---

## Hooks System

Configure in `~/.claude/settings.json` under `"hooks"`. Personal hooks live in
`~/.claude/personal/hooks/` (symlink to `users/emarquez-personal/hooks/`).

| Event | Fires When | Good For |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| `PreToolUse` | Before any tool call | Validation, safety checks |
| `PostToolUse` | After any tool call | Auto-format, notifications |
| `Stop` | Claude finishes responding | Checkpoint, post-session cleanup |

---

## Auto-Memory System

Persistent memory across sessions at `~/.claude/projects/{path}/memory/`.
Loaded automatically. Separate from `learning/` (memory = Claude's working knowledge; learning/ = curated knowledge base).

| Type | Content |
|------|---------|
| `user_*.md` | Who you are, expertise, preferences |
| `feedback_*.md` | Corrections and validated approaches |
| `project_*.md` | Ongoing work, goals, deadlines |
| `reference_*.md` | Where to find things externally |

`MEMORY.md` = index file. Keep under 200 lines.

---

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- Starting to code without reading existing code first
- Making "while I'm here" scope-creep changes
- Skipping verification (tests/lint) after changes
- Speculating about code behavior without reading it
- Large changes mixing unrelated concerns
- Writing comments that explain WHAT (code is self-documenting) — only write WHY
- Adding error handling for impossible scenarios
- Creating new files when editing existing ones works

---

## Git Workflow for This Repo

```bash
# After adding learnings, skills, or commands:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/  .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: [what you discovered]"
git push

# On another computer after pulling:
git pull
# No re-run of setup needed - symlinks already point here
```

---

## Maintenance Schedule

| Cadence | Action |
|---------|--------|
| **Weekly** | Review `learning/ideas/backlog.md`, capture week's learnings, commit |
| **Monthly** | Update personal/CLAUDE.md goals, archive stale content, review skills |
| **Quarterly** | Deep review, promote patterns to skills, plan next quarter |

---

## Learnings & Corrections

*Format: `[YYYY-MM-DD] Context: Learning.`*

- [2026-05-08] Architecture: `personal-claude-docs` is the shared personal baseline. `~/.claude/` is machine-specific. Obsidian vaults are OneDrive-only (not git). Symlinks connect all three.
- [2026-05-08] CLAUDE.md loading: `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` uses `@enterprise/CLAUDE.md` and `@personal/CLAUDE.md` to load shared content. Enterprise symlink at `~/.claude/enterprise` → `personal-claude-docs/.claude/enterprise/`.
- [2026-05-08] Hooks: Configure in `settings.json`. Fire on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop. Use for auto-format, git checkpoints, desktop notifications.

---

## Sources

- [Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow](https://x.com/bcherny/status/2007179832300581177)
- [Claude Code documentation](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/claude-code)
- [Dex Horthy / HumanLayer — Advanced Context Engineering](https://github.com/humanlayer/advanced-context-engineering-for-coding-agents)
- [12-Factor Agents](https://github.com/humanlayer/12-factor-agents)

# Personal Claude Environment - Eduardo Marquez

Last updated: 2026-05-08

This is your personal Claude Code configuration, separate from Safari Circuits work content.

---

## About This Setup

This repository provides:
- **Enterprise layer** - Reusable patterns across all your personal projects
- **User layer** - Eduardo-specific preferences and context
- **Git versioning** - Track how your Claude setup evolves
- **Multi-computer sync** - Same setup on home and other personal computers

**Source:** `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs`

---

## Core Principles

1. **Capture as you learn** - Document insights when you discover them
2. **Build reusable skills** - Turn repeated patterns into skills
3. **Keep it simple** - Minimal structure, maximum value
4. **Version everything** - Commit learnings and skills to git
5. **Evolve continuously** - Review and refine weekly/monthly

---

## Folder Structure

```
.claude/enterprise/              (Reusable across all personal projects)
├── learning/
│   ├── best-practices/         (Validated knowledge)
│   └── ideas/                  (Idea backlog)
├── skills/                     (Reusable workflows)
├── commands/                   (Custom slash commands)
├── reference/                  (Quick lookup docs)
└── agents/                     (AI subagents)

.claude/users/emarquez-personal/ (Personal preferences)
├── skills/                     (Personal-only skills)
├── commands/                   (Personal-only commands)
└── hooks/                      (Custom hooks)
```

---

## My Preferences

### Work Style

**Decision-Making:**
- Present 2-3 options with clear tradeoffs
- Flag risks proactively
- I prefer "here's what could go wrong" over optimistic estimates

**Communication:**
- Brief summaries first, details on request
- Use tables for comparisons
- Technical depth is fine - I'm hands-on

**Time & Focus:**
- Work in 1-2 hour focused blocks
- Context switches are normal
- End-of-day: prefer quick wins over starting large tasks

**Things I Value:**
- Clear tradeoff explanations
- Proactive risk identification
- Learning from mistakes (capture in learnings)
- Incremental progress over big-bang changes

**Things That Frustrate Me:**
- Surprises in production
- Documentation drift
- Over-engineering when simple works

---

## Technologies I Use

### Languages
- **Primary:** Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, SQL
- **Learning:** Go, Rust (future)

### Frameworks & Libraries
- **Backend:** FastAPI, Express, Flask
- **Frontend:** React, Next.js
- **Data:** Pandas, NumPy

### Databases
- **SQL:** PostgreSQL, SQL Server
- **NoSQL:** MongoDB, Redis

### Cloud & Infrastructure
- **Cloud:** Azure (primary), AWS (learning)
- **Containers:** Docker, Docker Compose
- **IaC:** Terraform (learning)

### Tools
- **Editor:** VS Code, Claude Code
- **Version Control:** Git, GitHub
- **Notes:** Obsidian
- **Terminal:** PowerShell, bash

---

## Common Tasks

### Things I Build Often
- REST APIs with authentication
- Data processing pipelines
- Admin dashboards
- Database schemas and migrations
- Automation scripts

### Patterns I Use
- Repository pattern for data access
- Dependency injection
- API versioning
- Error handling middleware
- Database transactions

---

## Learning Goals (2026)

**Current Focus:**
- [ ] Advanced TypeScript patterns
- [ ] Docker orchestration and Kubernetes basics
- [ ] System design patterns
- [ ] Performance optimization techniques

**Future Quarters:**
- [ ] Go for backend services
- [ ] GraphQL APIs
- [ ] Event-driven architecture
- [ ] Distributed systems patterns

---

## Project Types

### Personal Projects
- Side projects and experiments
- Learning exercises
- Open source contributions
- Personal automation tools

### Home Lab
- Home server setup
- Network automation
- Self-hosted services
- Infrastructure experiments

### Study & Research
- Technology research
- API explorations
- Performance testing
- Architecture design practice

---

## Coding Standards (Personal)

### General Principles
- **Readability first** - Code is read more than written
- **Simple over clever** - Clever code is hard to maintain
- **Test what matters** - Focus on business logic, not trivial code
- **Document why, not what** - Code shows what, comments explain why

### TypeScript/JavaScript
- Use `type` over `interface`
- Prefer functional patterns where appropriate
- Use `const` by default, `let` when needed, never `var`
- Avoid `any` - use `unknown` if type is truly unknown

### Python
- Follow PEP 8
- Type hints on functions (params + return)
- Use dataclasses for data structures
- Prefer list comprehensions for simple transformations

### SQL
- Use explicit JOINs (never implicit)
- Always use transactions for multi-statement operations
- Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN
- Comment complex queries

### Git
- Commit messages: imperative mood ("Add feature" not "Added feature")
- Small, focused commits
- Branch naming: `feature/`, `fix/`, `refactor/`
- Rebase before merge to keep history clean

---

## Custom Commands

*(Add your custom /commands as you create them)*

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/review` | Review code changes before commit |
| `/learn` | Capture a learning in best-practices/ |
| `/quickfix` | Quick bug fix workflow |

---

## Maintenance Schedule

### Weekly (Friday Evening)
- Review `learning/ideas/backlog.md`
- Capture any learnings from the week
- Update skills that evolved
- Check learning goals progress

### Monthly (First Weekend)
- Review and update this CLAUDE.md
- Archive obsolete content
- Update learning goals
- Review project progress

### Quarterly
- Deep review of all learnings
- Promote patterns to skills
- Update technology focus areas
- Plan next quarter's goals

---

## Claude Behavior

### Auto-Capture Patterns

When I say these phrases, help me capture knowledge:
- "I learned that..." → Suggest capturing in learning/best-practices/
- "I keep doing..." → Suggest creating a skill
- "Next time I should..." → Capture in ideas/backlog.md

### Correction Handling

When I correct you:
1. Acknowledge the correction
2. Offer to capture it as a learning
3. Apply it going forward

### Workflow Assistance

- **Planning:** Help break down large tasks into steps
- **Research:** Explore technologies/patterns before building
- **Review:** Check my code for issues before commit
- **Learning:** Help document what I discover

---

## Context Review

Use these prompts for regular reviews:

### Weekly Review
```
Let's do my weekly review:
1. What learnings should I capture from this week?
2. Any skills that need updating?
3. Check learning goals progress
```

### Monthly Review
```
Monthly Claude environment review:
1. Review learning/ideas/backlog.md
2. Update CLAUDE.md preferences
3. Archive obsolete content
4. Update learning goals
```

---

## Notes for Claude

- This is personal use only (no team/work content)
- I'm building this system as I go - suggestions welcome
- When I correct you, auto-propose capturing the learning
- Focus on practical, reusable knowledge
- Keep things simple and maintainable

---

## Repository Management

**Git workflow:**
- Commit learnings and skills as they're created
- Use descriptive commit messages
- Tag major milestones (quarterly reviews, significant additions)

**Branching:**
- `main` - stable, tested content
- `dev` - work in progress
- `experiment/` - trying new patterns

**Backup:**
- Push to GitHub regularly
- This repo syncs to all personal computers via git

---

## Getting Started Checklist

- [x] Repository created and initialized
- [x] Folder structure in place
- [x] CLAUDE.md created
- [ ] First learning captured
- [ ] First skill created
- [ ] First command added
- [ ] Bootstrap script run on home computer
- [ ] Pushed to GitHub

---

## Sources & Inspiration

- Safari Circuits team standards (organizational-docs)
- Boris Cherny's Claude Code workflow
- HumanLayer's Context Engineering patterns
- Personal experience and preferences

> Loaded by `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` via `@personal/CLAUDE.md` on every session.
> Contains Eduardo-specific preferences that override or supplement the enterprise layer.

---

## Work Style & Communication

**Decision-Making:**
- Present 2–3 options with clear tradeoffs; let me choose
- Flag risks proactively — "here's what could go wrong" over optimistic estimates
- I prefer explicit uncertainty over false confidence

**Communication:**
- Start with brief summary, provide details on request
- Use tables for comparisons — easier to scan
- Technical depth is fine — I'm hands-on when needed
- No emojis unless explicitly requested

**Time & Focus:**
- Work in 1–2 hour focused blocks
- Context switches between priorities are normal
- End-of-day: prefer quick wins over starting large tasks

- Incremental progress over big-bang changes
- Learning from mistakes — always capture corrections

**What Frustrates Me:**
- Surprises (always confirm before irreversible actions)
- Documentation drift (update docs WITH code changes)
- Over-engineering when simple works
- Silent failures or skipped verification

---

---

## Technology Preferences

### Languages

- **Learning:** Go, Rust

### Code Standards

- Avoid `any` — use `unknown` when type is truly unknown
- Prefer functional patterns where appropriate

**Python:**

- Dataclasses for data structures
- List comprehensions for simple transformations

**SQL:**

- Always use transactions for multi-statement operations
- Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN
- Flag query performance implications

**General:**
- Read existing code first, match patterns
- Minimal and focused changes
- Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
- No multi-paragraph docstrings

---

## Current Focus Areas

> Review monthly — update when priorities shift.

**Active (as of 2026-05-08):**
- [ ] Personal Claude Code environment (this repo)
- [ ] Advanced TypeScript patterns
- [ ] Docker orchestration basics
- [ ] System design patterns

# Personal Preferences — Eduardo Marquez

> Loaded by `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` via `@personal/CLAUDE.md` on every session.
> Contains Eduardo-specific preferences that override or supplement the enterprise layer.

---

## Work Style & Communication

**Decision-Making:**
- Present 2–3 options with clear tradeoffs; let me choose
- Flag risks proactively — "here's what could go wrong" over optimistic estimates
- I prefer explicit uncertainty over false confidence

**Communication:**
- Start with brief summary, provide details on request
- Use tables for comparisons — easier to scan
- Technical depth is fine — I'm hands-on when needed
- No emojis unless explicitly requested

**Time & Focus:**
- Work in 1–2 hour focused blocks
- Context switches between priorities are normal
- End-of-day: prefer quick wins over starting large tasks

**What I Value:**
- Clear tradeoff explanations
- Proactive risk identification
- Documentation as you build (not after)
- Incremental progress over big-bang changes
- Learning from mistakes — always capture corrections

**What Frustrates Me:**
- Surprises (always confirm before irreversible actions)
- Documentation drift (update docs WITH code changes)
- Over-engineering when simple works
- Silent failures or skipped verification

---

## Role & Context

**Day job:** IT Manager, Safari Circuits
- BeFirst platform (Odoo-based), Azure migration, Claude Code team adoption
- Reports to Scott (IT Director); key stakeholders: Julien (Operations), Arturo (Manufacturing)

**Personal focus:** Learning, side projects, home lab, open source
- Work context is in ClaudeWork repo; this layer applies everywhere

---

## Technology Preferences

### Languages
- **Primary:** Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, SQL
- **Learning:** Go, Rust

### Stack
- **Backend:** FastAPI, Express, Flask
- **Frontend:** React, Next.js
- **Data:** Pandas, NumPy, SQL Server, PostgreSQL
- **Cloud:** Azure (primary), AWS (learning)
- **Containers:** Docker, Docker Compose

### Code Standards

**TypeScript/JavaScript:**
- `type` over `interface`
- `const` by default, `let` when needed, never `var`
- Avoid `any` — use `unknown` when type is truly unknown
- Prefer functional patterns where appropriate

**Python:**
- PEP 8
- Type hints on all functions (params + return)
- Dataclasses for data structures
- List comprehensions for simple transformations

**SQL:**
- Explicit JOINs (never implicit)
- Always use transactions for multi-statement operations
- Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN
- Flag query performance implications

**General:**
- Read existing code first, match patterns
- Minimal and focused changes
- Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
- No multi-paragraph docstrings

---

input.content:
# personal-claude-docs

Personal Claude Code knowledge base. Shared across all computers via GitHub.

## Architecture

```
personal-claude-docs/          ← This repo (git, portable)
  .claude/enterprise/          ← Shared config: commands, skills, learning, reference
  .claude/users/emarquez-personal/  ← Personal preferences and overrides
  .claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1  ← Run once per machine

~/.claude/                     ← Machine repo (ClaudeHome / ClaudeWork)
  CLAUDE.md                    ← Thin loader: @enterprise + @personal + machine paths
  commands/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/commands/
  skills/    ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/skills/
  learning/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/learning/
  reference/ ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/reference/
  agents/    ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/agents/
  enterprise/──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/   (enables @enterprise/ imports)
  personal/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/users/emarquez-personal/

Obsidian vault (OneDrive)      ← Editing environment, NOT git
  04-Commands/_claude-commands ──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/commands/
  03-Skills/_claude-skills     ──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/skills/
  02-Learnings/_claude-learning──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/learning/
  05-Reference/_claude-reference──symlink─→ .claude/enterprise/reference/
```

## Computers

| Computer | User | OneDrive | Claude repo |
|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | — |

On **work computer**: ClaudeWork `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` loads enterprise + personal layers,
then adds Safari Circuits / work-specific context. Personal config always applies first.

## New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone this repo
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# 2. Clone your machine repo (home or work)
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
# or: git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeWork.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"

# 3. Run bootstrap (auto-detects home vs work)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 4. Open Claude Code - your full personal context loads automatically
claude
```

## Daily Workflow

```powershell
# After adding a learning or skill:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: what you discovered"
git push

# On another machine:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git pull
# No re-run needed - symlinks already point here
```

## Folder Guide

| Folder | Purpose | How to add |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Add `{name}.md` |
| `enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Add `{name}/SKILL.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/best-practices/` | Validated knowledge | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Quick capture | Append to file |
| `enterprise/reference/` | Quick lookup docs | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Add `{name}.md` |
| `users/emarquez-personal/` | Personal overrides | Edit CLAUDE.md |

aiTitle: Set up personal Claude Code environment structure

content.text:
Good question. Let me lay out the two approaches so you can decide:

---

**Option A — Current (vault → personal-claude-docs directly)**
```
01_Claude_Home/_claude-commands  →  personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands/
~/.claude/commands               →  personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands/
```
Both point to the same real dir independently. Two hops from different starting points.

---

**Option B — Vault wired to `~/.claude/` (one level of indirection)**
```
01_Claude_Home/_claude-commands  →  ~/.claude/commands  →  personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands/
~/.claude/commands               →  personal-claude-docs/enterprise/commands/
```
Obsidian mirrors exactly what Claude sees. If the Claude wiring ever changes, Obsidian follows automatically.

---

**Option C — Vault wiring belongs in ClaudeHome / ClaudeWork (not this repo)**
```
personal-claude-docs/setup-personal.ps1  →  wires ~/.claude/ only
ClaudeHome setup script                  →  wires 01_Claude_Home/_claude-* → ~/.claude/
ClaudeWork setup script                  →  wires 00_Claude/_claude-*     → ~/.claude/
```
Clean separation: this repo only knows about personal content, each machine repo knows which vault to wire.

---

**My recommendation: Option C.**

Each computer's Claude repo (`ClaudeHome` / `ClaudeWork`) owns its own vault wiring. On home, `ClaudeHome` wires `01_Claude_Home`. On work, `ClaudeWork` wires `00_Claude`. The `personal-claude-docs` script stays focused on content — it doesn't need to know about vaults at all.

Which approach do you want?

input.content:
# personal-claude-docs

Personal Claude Code knowledge base. Shared across all computers via GitHub.

---

## Architecture

```
GIT REPOS                        CONTENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ClaudeDocs                       enterprise/ commands, skills,
(personal-claude-docs)      ──►  learning, reference, agents
                                 users/emarquez-personal/ prefs

ClaudeHome                       CLAUDE.md (home loader)
(~/.claude/ on home)        ──►  @enterprise + @personal + home paths
                                 settings.json, memory/

ClaudeWork                       CLAUDE.md (work loader)
(~/.claude/ on work)        ──►  @enterprise + @personal + work context
                                 settings.json, memory/

ONEDRIVE (same personal account - both computers)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
01_Claude_Home/                  Personal Obsidian vault
00_Claude/                       Work Obsidian vault
```

```
HOME COMPUTER  (eduar / D:\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeHome repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)             │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

WORK COMPUTER  (emarquez / C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeWork repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)                  │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Setup Script Ownership

| Script | Lives in | Responsibility |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| `setup-personal.ps1` | ClaudeDocs | Wire `~/.claude/` → ClaudeDocs content only |
| `setup-home.ps1` | ClaudeHome (`~/.claude/.setup/`) | Call setup-personal + wire `01_Claude_Home` → `~/.claude/` |
| `setup-work.ps1` | ClaudeWork (`~/.claude/.setup/`) | Call setup-personal + wire `00_Claude` → `~/.claude/` |

### CLAUDE.md Loading Order (every session)

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  @enterprise/CLAUDE.md   ←  Boris Cherny practices, workflow (this repo)
  @personal/CLAUDE.md     ←  Eduardo's preferences, tech stack (this repo)
  ## Machine section       ←  local paths only
```

On work computer, the work-specific section (Safari Circuits, ADO, BeFirst) follows the personal layers.

---

## Computers

| Computer | User | OneDrive | Active Vault | Claude repo |
|----------|------|----------|--------------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | `01_Claude_Home` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | `00_Claude` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | `01_Claude_Home` | ClaudeHome or new |

Both vaults (`01_Claude_Home` and `00_Claude`) exist on **every computer** via OneDrive sync.
Only the active vault for that computer is wired to `~/.claude/`.

---

## New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone repos
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# Home computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-home.ps1"

# Work computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeWork.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-work.ps1"
```

> `setup-home.ps1` and `setup-work.ps1` each call `setup-personal.ps1` internally,
> then wire their respective vault. One script, full setup.

---

## Daily Workflow

```powershell
# After adding a learning, skill, or command:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: what you discovered"
git push

# On another computer:
git pull
# No re-run needed - symlinks already point here
```

---

## Folder Guide

| Folder | Purpose | How to add |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Add `{name}.md` |
| `enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Add `{name}/SKILL.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/best-practices/` | Validated knowledge | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Quick capture | Append to file |
| `enterprise/reference/` | Quick lookup docs | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Add `{name}.md` |
| `users/emarquez-personal/` | Personal overrides | Edit CLAUDE.md |
| `scripts/setup-personal.ps1` | Wire `~/.claude/` | Run from ClaudeHome/Work setup |
| `scripts/setup-work-template.ps1` | Template for ClaudeWork | Copy to ClaudeWork `.setup/` |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# personal-claude-docs

Personal Claude Code knowledge base. Shared across all computers via GitHub.

## Architecture

```
personal-claude-docs/          ← This repo (git, portable)
  .claude/enterprise/          ← Shared config: commands, skills, learning, reference
  .claude/users/emarquez-personal/  ← Personal preferences and overrides
  .claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1  ← Run once per machine

~/.claude/                     ← Machine repo (ClaudeHome / ClaudeWork)
  CLAUDE.md                    ← Thin loader: @enterprise + @personal + machine paths
  commands/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/commands/
  skills/    ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/skills/
  learning/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/learning/
  reference/ ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/reference/
  agents/    ──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/agents/
  enterprise/──symlink──────→  .claude/enterprise/   (enables @enterprise/ imports)
  personal/  ──symlink──────→  .claude/users/emarquez-personal/

Obsidian vault (OneDrive)      ← Editing environment, NOT git
  04-Commands/_claude-commands ──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/commands/
  03-Skills/_claude-skills     ──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/skills/
  02-Learnings/_claude-learning──symlink──→ .claude/enterprise/learning/
  05-Reference/_claude-reference──symlink─→ .claude/enterprise/reference/
```

## Computers

| Computer | User | OneDrive | Claude repo |
|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | — |

On **work computer**: ClaudeWork `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` loads enterprise + personal layers,
then adds Safari Circuits / work-specific context. Personal config always applies first.

## New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone this repo
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# 2. Clone your machine repo (home or work)
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
# or: git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeWork.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"

# 3. Run bootstrap (auto-detects home vs work)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 4. Open Claude Code - your full personal context loads automatically
claude
```

## Daily Workflow

```powershell
# After adding a learning or skill:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git add .claude/enterprise/ .claude/users/
git commit -m "Learn: what you discovered"
git push

# On another machine:
cd ~/personal-claude-docs
git pull
# No re-run needed - symlinks already point here
```

## Folder Guide

| Folder | Purpose | How to add |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `enterprise/commands/` | Custom `/commands` | Add `{name}.md` |
| `enterprise/skills/` | Reusable workflows | Add `{name}/SKILL.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/best-practices/` | Validated knowledge | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/learning/ideas/backlog.md` | Quick capture | Append to file |
| `enterprise/reference/` | Quick lookup docs | Add `{topic}.md` |
| `enterprise/agents/` | Subagent definitions | Add `{name}.md` |
| `users/emarquez-personal/` | Personal overrides | Edit CLAUDE.md |

Each machine has a thin `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` that loads this file and the personal layer.

```
ClaudeDocs repo (personal-claude-docs/)
├── .claude/enterprise/CLAUDE.md    ← This file. Portable, machine-agnostic.

├── .claude/enterprise/learning/    ← ~/.claude/learning → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/reference/   ← ~/.claude/reference → symlink → here
├── .claude/enterprise/agents/      ← ~/.claude/agents → symlink → here
├── .claude/users/emarquez-personal/CLAUDE.md  ← Personal preferences layer
└── .claude/scripts/setup-personal.ps1         ← Run once per machine
```

### Loading Order (every Claude session)

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  @enterprise/CLAUDE.md   ← this file  (Boris Cherny practices, workflow)
  @personal/CLAUDE.md     ← personal preferences, tech stack, current focus
  ## Machine-specific     ← local paths, nothing else
```

### Machines

| Machine | User | OneDrive | ClaudeDocs | Claude repo |
|---------|------|----------|------------|-------------|
| Home | `eduar` | `D:\OneDrive\` | `C:\Users\eduar\personal-claude-docs\` | ClaudeHome |
| Work | `emarquez` | `C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\` | `C:\Users\emarquez\personal-claude-docs\` | ClaudeWork |
| Laptop | `{user}` | auto-detect | `C:\Users\{user}\personal-claude-docs\` | — |

### New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone repos
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"   # or ClaudeWork

# 2. Run bootstrap (auto-detects home vs work by OneDrive location)
& "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs\.claude\scripts\setup-personal.ps1"

# 3. Verify
claude  # CLAUDE.md loads, commands/skills available
```

### Work Computer Layering

On the work computer, `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (ClaudeWork repo) loads:

2. `@personal/CLAUDE.md` — personal preferences

## Multi-Computer Architecture

This configuration is shared across all personal computers through `ClaudeDocs`.
Each machine has a thin `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` that loads this file and the personal layer.

```
HOME COMPUTER  (eduar / D:\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeHome repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)             │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

WORK COMPUTER  (emarquez / C:\Users\emarquez\OneDrive\)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ~/.claude/  (ClaudeWork repo)                       │
│    commands/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/commands
│    skills/   ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/skills
│    learning/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/learning
│    reference/──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/reference
│    enterprise/─symlink──► ClaudeDocs/enterprise/
│    personal/ ──symlink──► ClaudeDocs/users/emarquez-personal/
│                                                      │
│  00_Claude/  (ACTIVE vault - wired)                  │
│    _claude-commands ──symlink──► ~/.claude/commands  │
│    _claude-skills   ──symlink──► ~/.claude/skills    │
│    _claude-learning ──symlink──► ~/.claude/learning  │
│    _claude-reference──symlink──► ~/.claude/reference │
│                                                      │
│  01_Claude_Home/  (on disk via OneDrive, NOT wired)  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Both vaults (`01_Claude_Home` and `00_Claude`) exist on every computer via OneDrive.
Only the active vault for that computer is wired.

### Loading Order (every Claude session)

```
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md
  @enterprise/CLAUDE.md   ← this file  (Boris Cherny practices, workflow)
  @personal/CLAUDE.md     ← personal preferences, tech stack, current focus
  ## Machine-specific     ← local paths, nothing else
```

On work computer the work-specific section (Safari Circuits, ADO, BeFirst) follows — personal context always loads first.

### Setup Script Ownership

| Script | Lives in | Responsibility |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| `setup-personal.ps1` | ClaudeDocs | Wire `~/.claude/` → ClaudeDocs only |
| `setup-home.ps1` | ClaudeHome `.setup/` | Call setup-personal + wire `01_Claude_Home` → `~/.claude/` |
| `setup-work.ps1` | ClaudeWork `.setup/` | Call setup-personal + wire `00_Claude` → `~/.claude/` |

### New Machine Setup

```powershell
# 1. Clone repos
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeDocs.git "$env:USERPROFILE\personal-claude-docs"

# Home computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeHome.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-home.ps1"

# Work computer
git clone https://github.com/Eduardodmb/ClaudeWork.git "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude"
& "$env:USERPROFILE\.claude\.setup\setup-work.ps1"
```

content.text:
Exactly — and the architecture supports exactly that. Every session on any computer:

1. **Reads** the same enterprise practices and personal preferences (ClaudeDocs via symlinks)
2. **Adds to** the same knowledge base — commit a learning on home, pull on work, it's there
3. **Edits** naturally in Obsidian without thinking about the underlying structure

The knowledge compounds over time: ideas captured in `learning/ideas/backlog.md` get promoted to `best-practices/`, patterns become `skills/`, repeated workflows become `/commands`. The pipeline runs itself as long as you commit regularly.
